samples.xml_description:
Implement a series-parallel conversion circuit. It receives a serial input signal "din_serial" along with a control signal "din_valid" indicating the validity of the input data. The module operates on the rising edge of the clock signal "clk" and uses a synchronous design. The input din_serial is a single-bit data, and when the module receives 8 input data, the output dout_parallel outputs the 8-bit data(The serial input values are sequentially placed in dout_parallel from the most significant bit to the least significant bit), and the dout_valid is set to 1. Clock signal. Reset signal (active low). Serial input data. Validity signal for input data. Parallel output data. Validity signal for the output data. ADD SUB MULT ACC NAND LT Counter for the number of serial input data received. Temporary register for parallel output data. Validity signal for the output data.

Initialize the counter, output data, and output validity to 0.

When the reset signal is not active and the validity of the serial input data is 1:

  • Increment the counter.
  • Place the serial input data in the corresponding position of the parallel output data.

When the counter reaches 7:

  • Output the parallel output data.
  • Set the validity signal of the output data to 1.
  • Reset the counter.

When the validity of the serial input data is 0:

  • Reset the counter.
  • Set the validity signal of the output data to 0.
